Home/Payment Infrastructure & CBDC
CLEARING & SETTLEMENT · CBDC SMART CONTRACTS · CENTRAL BANK SYSTEMS · CROSS-BORDER

Payment Infrastructure
& CBDC

From national instant payment clearing systems for central banks, to China's first verified live CBDC smart contract banking application — Yusys brings proven payment infrastructure capability and programmable digital currency expertise to the global market.

Overview Central Bank Systems CBDC Smart Contracts Cross-Border
Capability Overview

Proven infrastructure. Programmable currency.

Yusys brings together two areas of deep, production-verified expertise: the clearing and settlement infrastructure that central banks depend on, and the CBDC smart contract layer that makes digital money programmable. Both are built on real deployments — not pilots or whitepapers.

For economies where improving payment efficiency is the clear immediate priority, we bring proven infrastructure. For institutions ready to explore digital currency, we bring a live smart contract blueprint.

🏛
CAPABILITY 01
Central Bank Payment Infrastructure

Design and build national-level instant payment clearing systems — the infrastructure central banks use to process interbank transfers, manage settlement, and serve the entire economy 24/7.

✓Real-time interbank clearing & settlement
✓Alias directory & e-wallet interoperability
✓KYC/AML & risk control embedded
✓Comparable to PIX, UPI, FedNow
🪙
CAPABILITY 02
CBDC & Programmable Money

One of the first fintech vendors in China with a verified live CBDC smart contract deployment — covering digital wallet lifecycle and programmable consumer scenarios built on central bank digital currency rails.

✓Live smart contract: Red Packet system
✓Full CBDC wallet lifecycle
✓CIPS & mBridge cross-border ready
✓BRICS-certified payment service provider
What makes Yusys different

Most vendors can describe payment architecture. Yusys has built it in production — operating national-level clearing infrastructure that processes hundreds of millions of daily transactions, and deploying live CBDC smart contracts inside a real central bank digital currency system.

110M+
Daily txns (clearing)
24/7
High-concurrency operations
1st
Live CBDC smart contract
Central Bank Systems

Designing the infrastructure
that powers a nation's payments.

Yusys has the architecture, methodology, and experience to design and deliver national-level instant payment systems — the kind that central banks use to modernize their country's payment infrastructure.

Our approach is modelled on proven global systems — Brazil's PIX, India's UPI, the U.S. FedNow — and draws on China's own systematic evolution from traditional banking systems to real-time payment networks and digital currency pilots.

Global Reference Systems
🇧🇷Brazil PIX
🇮🇳India UPI
🇺🇸U.S. FedNow
🇨🇷Costa Rica Sinpe Móvil
⚡
Real-Time Instant Payment Processing

24/7/365 platform processing interbank transfers and e-wallet transactions in real time — scalable from 20 to 200+ TPS as adoption grows. Supports both local currency and USD.

🌐
International Standards Compliance

Fully compliant with international messaging and communication standards — ensuring interoperability with global payment networks and readiness for cross-border expansion.

📋
Alias Directory & Wallet Interoperability

Centrally managed alias directory (phone number, email) links bank accounts and e-wallets across all participating institutions — enabling pay-by-alias without sharing sensitive account details.

🛡
KYC/AML & Multi-Layer Risk Control

Blacklist management, transaction blocking, and AML monitoring embedded at the architecture level. TLS 1.3 encryption, multi-factor authentication (biometric + certificate), and VPN isolation for all participants.

Proven Implementation Methodology
Phased rollout — from core platform to regional interconnection
PHASE 1 · YEAR 1–2
Core Platform & Institution Onboarding

Build the central payment platform and connect initial financial institutions. Enable interbank and e-wallet transfers. Complete testing with first-batch participants.

PHASE 2 · YEAR 2–3
Expanded Payment Functions

Add payment request processing, QR code payments, and bulk payments. Expand connected institution coverage and optimize system performance and user experience.

PHASE 3 · YEAR 3–4
Cross-Border Payment Integration

Integrate international payment channels. Enable cross-border transactions, dispute resolution, and settlement mechanisms aligned with regional standards.

PHASE 4 · YEAR 4+
Regional Interconnection & Modernization

Cross-country payment system interoperability. Support for advanced financial services innovation and preparation for next-generation infrastructure.

Delivery model: On-site + Remote · Development: Agile iterative · Standards: International · Enterprise-grade HA
🏆 China's First Verified Live CBDC Smart Contract Banking Application

The Red Packet Smart Contract.

At a time when most fintech vendors remained at whitepaper or pilot stage, Yusys designed, built, and deployed a live smart contract system inside a real central bank digital currency infrastructure — and it went into production.

What was built

Consumer Red Packet Smart Contract Subsystem

Deployed as a production subsystem within an overseas central bank digital currency project. Enables programmable, conditional disbursement of digital currency — where the money itself carries rules about how, when, and where it can be spent.

This is not a concept demonstration. This is a live system, running on real CBDC rails, serving real users.

🎯
DIRECTED SPENDING
Merchant & Category Restrictions

Digital currency locked to specific merchants or spending categories, valid only within defined time windows — programmatically eliminating subsidy fraud and cash-out risk at the protocol level.

📈
PROGRAMMATIC INCENTIVES
Tiered Cashback & Graduated Rewards

Smart contracts automatically trigger graduated cashback rewards as spend thresholds are crossed — zero manual reconciliation, zero leakage, zero human execution delay.

⏱
TIME-LIMITED VALIDITY
Expiry & Reclaim Logic

Unspent digital currency automatically reclaimed at expiry. No manual follow-up, no dormant float liability — the contract enforces every rule autonomously on-chain.

🏛
Central Bank Grade

Deployed inside a real central bank CBDC infrastructure — not a sandbox, not a prototype. The security, reliability, and compliance requirements are the highest possible.

🌍
International Context

Built for an overseas digital currency project — proving that Yusys's smart contract architecture works across regulatory jurisdictions, not just in China's domestic e-CNY ecosystem.

🔓
Blueprint for the Industry

This deployment establishes a proven design pattern for programmable CBDC consumer scenarios — a replicable blueprint for central banks and financial institutions exploring digital currency applications.

Cross-Border & Global Outlook

From domestic rails to
international corridors.

Yusys's payment infrastructure capability extends far beyond domestic China — connecting banks to international clearing networks, multi-CBDC bridges, and next-generation cross-border settlement corridors.

🌐
CIPS Cross-Border Access

Technical integration services for banks connecting to CIPS — China's cross-border RMB clearing and settlement infrastructure — including frontend access gateways and internal business logic modules.

✓ Successfully enabled CIPS access for multiple commercial and agricultural banks
🤝
mBridge & BRICS Payments

As the first officially certified BRICS cross-border payment system service provider, Yusys helps banks participate in multi-CBDC bridge (mBridge) corridors — enabling efficient, low-cost cross-border settlement bypassing traditional correspondent banking.

✓ First certified BRICS cross-border payment system service provider
🤝
Industry Standard Setting
Actively participating in digital currency and payment technical standards development — Yusys shapes the rules, not just follows them.
⛓
Supply Chain Finance
Smart contracts enabling multi-tier credit penetration through supply chains — giving SME suppliers faster, cheaper access to financing backed by core enterprise credit.
🔬
Stablecoin & Web3 Research
In partnership with the Macau Science and Technology Fund, researching next-generation Web3 retail payment systems and stablecoin-CBDC infrastructure for cross-border retail use cases.

Ready to build for the payment infrastructure era?

Whether you're a central bank designing a national instant payment system, or a bank preparing to explore CBDC smart contracts — Yusys brings proven architecture, methodology, and a live production track record to your project.

Home/AI Platform
AI FOR FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS · MULTI-AGENT APPLICATIONS

AI built for financial institutions.
From strategy to production.

Yusys AI is not a single product — it's a full-stack capability layer spanning infrastructure, knowledge management, and four distinct application lines: retail banking, lending operations, data intelligence, and enterprise AI enablement. All built on a financial-grade LLM architecture supporting domestic and global models.

Four AI Application Lines

One platform. Four ways it works for banks.

Each application line is purpose-built for a distinct banking workflow — designed to be deployed independently or as an integrated AI strategy across the institution.

🤖
AI-Powered Mobile Banking Super App

Multi-agent retail banking assistant acting as Friend, Housekeeper, and Concierge — delivering hyper-personalized services to 100% of customers through a single mobile entry point. Powered by 6 specialized agents.

6 Agents Super App Retail Banking
→ Full product details
🔍
AI Due Diligence Engine

Four intelligent agents automate the complete loan due diligence pipeline — from document ingestion to audit-ready report. 2 business days → 4 hours. 97% classification accuracy. The 80/20 rule: AI handles 80% of standardized work, humans focus on judgment.

Days → Hours
faster turnaround
80%↓
cost reduction
→ Full product details
🏦
AI for Smart Lending Operations

End-to-end AI system covering the complete lending lifecycle — from origination to collections. Process orchestration, information acquisition, financial decision analytics, and automated report generation across due diligence, credit approval, loan monitoring, and write-off.

70% Productivity↑ 90%+ Quality 500%+ Productivity Gain
Full lending lifecycle: Origination → Servicing → Collection & Recovery
📊
ChatBI — Conversational Data Intelligence

Natural language interface to all banking data — business users query, analyze, and generate reports without SQL or data team dependency. Covers Growth Analysis, Risk Monitor, KPI Decomposition, Customer Analysis, Churn Alert, and Operational Efficiency scenarios.

Natural Language Query 7 Business Scenarios
→ Part of Enterprise Data Intelligence
Technical Foundation

Model-agnostic. Deployment-flexible. Compliance-first.

Yusys's AI infrastructure supports every major domestic Chinese and international large language model — giving banks the flexibility to choose models that meet their regulatory requirements, data residency rules, and performance needs. All AI workloads run on fully compliant, financial-grade infrastructure with complete data sovereignty.

Knowledge Platform

From individual expertise
to institutional capital.

Most banks face a critical knowledge problem: the expertise that drives decisions lives in senior people's heads — not in systems. When they leave, the knowledge leaves with them.

Yusys's Knowledge Platform converts tacit expert knowledge into institutional AI-ready digital assets — creating a self-reinforcing flywheel where every decision improves the system.

THE KNOWLEDGE FLYWHEEL
1
Knowledge extraction from experts
2
AI-ready knowledge base construction
3
Agent-driven decision application
4
Feedback loop refines the knowledge base
🎤
AI-Assisted Knowledge Extraction

Intelligent interview scripting and conversational mapping capture expert heuristics on-the-fly — far more efficient than documentation sessions, and captures the tacit knowledge traditional documentation misses.

📚
RAG-Enhanced Knowledge Base

Automated document parsing — SOPs, regulatory documents, operation manuals — structured into knowledge nodes and indexed for retrieval-augmented generation. Agents access institutional knowledge on demand.

🧠
Agent-Knowledge-Driven Decision Making

AI agents ground decisions in the institutional knowledge base — reducing model hallucination and increasing interpretability and traceability. Expert-level outputs, available to every account manager, not just the top performers.

🎓
Intelligent Mentorship for New Hires

End-to-end guidance framework (Outline → Navigation → Best Practices → Drafting → Evaluation) reduces new hire training cycles by 50% — compressing 6-month onboarding curves significantly.

Implementation Methodology

Yusys is a strategic AI partner,
not just a vendor.

Successful AI deployment requires an integrated understanding of business, product, engineering, algorithms, and data. Yusys provides end-to-end AI enablement services across the full value chain.

🗺️
AI Business Enablement Planning

Balancing cost, security, and sustainability — Yusys helps institutions identify the highest-value AI scenarios, design a phased adoption roadmap, and run scenario-based pilot programs before enterprise-wide rollout.

  • ✓AI scenario identification & prioritization
  • ✓Computing resource optimization
  • ✓Pilot program design & validation
⚙️
End-to-End AI Intelligent Services

Full-stack technical delivery — from infrastructure architecture and knowledge lifecycle planning to agent platform implementation and integration with existing banking systems.

  • ✓Open & scalable agent architecture
  • ✓Integration with existing core systems
  • ✓Knowledge lifecycle planning & management
🔄
Continuous Evolution

AI is never "done." Yusys provides ongoing technology foresight, monitoring of AI advancement, and real-time strategy pivots — moving institutions from fixed plans to agile, continuously evolving AI capabilities.

  • ✓Technology trend monitoring
  • ✓From fixed plans to agile evolution
  • ✓Cross-functional AI team building
THREE SCENARIO-SPECIFIC IMPLEMENTATION PATHS
Lightweight AI-for-Everyone

Foster AI culture across the organization through Agent Platform + broad adoption. Low barrier, high engagement, builds AI literacy bottom-up.

Knowledge Base Deployment

Knowledge Base product + content curation + scenario mapping. Converts institutional expertise into AI-ready assets that any agent can access.

Business-Critical Scenarios

Top-down strategic alignment first, then bottom-up innovation via pilots, followed by top-down resource allocation for enterprise-wide scale.

Ready to build your AI strategy?

Whether you're exploring AI for the first time or scaling a pilot to enterprise — Yusys provides the architecture, knowledge platform, and application expertise to go from strategy to production.

Home/About
About Yusys

27 years of
banking expertise.

From a software startup in Beijing in 1999 to a leading FinTech group listed on China's capital market (300674) — trusted by 1,000+ financial institutions across 10+ global markets.

Our Story

Technology that respects
the weight of money

Yusys Technologies was founded in Beijing in 1999 by engineers who believed banks deserved software built with the same rigor as the financial transactions it handles. We became one of IBM's top five service partners in China, and were recognized as one of the fastest-growing tech companies in the Asia-Pacific.

Listed on China's A-share market (Stock Code: 300674) in November 2018, we have been ranked the No. 1 IT solution provider in China's banking industry for 7 consecutive years by IDC — and No. 40 globally in IDC FinTech Rankings. With key partnerships with Huawei, Tencent, and Baidu, we drive an AI-enabled strategy across all banking solutions.

Yusys at a Glance
A-Share: 300674

Founded
9,000+
Employees
34+
Delivery centers
RMB 3.62B
Revenue (2025)

📍 Zhuhai, China
+ Hong Kong · Macao · Singapore · Indonesia · Cambodia · UK
Our Journey
1 / 23
Market Leadership

INSIGHT

Insights & Perspectives

Original perspectives on the forces reshaping financial technology.

From Aspiration to Execution: What It Really Takes to Scale AI in Banking

At Money20/20 Asia, Yusys Technologies' CTO and Hong Kong General Manager offered a ground-level view of where financial AI transformation actually stalls — and what it takes to move forward.

By Yusys Technologies Share in f 𝕏

BANGKOK, April 2026 — Ask any banking executive today whether their institution is investing in artificial intelligence, and the answer is almost universally yes. Ask whether that investment is delivering measurable business value at scale, and the conversation gets considerably quieter.

That gap — between AI ambition and AI execution — was the central thread running through Yusys Technologies' presence at Money20/20 Asia in Bangkok this April. Against the backdrop of a conference themed "From Infrastructure to Impact: Where Technology Meets Humanity," two leaders from the Company offered a candid and practical assessment of why the industry's AI transformation is moving more slowly than the headlines suggest — and what it genuinely takes to close the distance.

The Yusys Technologies booth at Money20/20 Asia 2026 in Bangkok drew sustained interest from financial institutions across Southeast Asia, Europe, and the Middle East.

The Operationalization Problem

Zhang Ning, Chief Technology Officer of Yusys Technologies, opened his panel with a reframe that set the tone for what followed. The question, he argued, is no longer whether banks should adopt AI. In China alone, more than 500 AI use cases have already been deployed across financial institutions, spanning customer service, investment research, and digital workforce automation. The industry has moved past proof-of-concept. The urgent question now is whether institutions can shift from experimenting with AI to operationalizing it — systematically, at scale, with real business outcomes attached.

Zhang Ning, CTO of Yusys Technologies, at Money20/20 Asia 2026

Three barriers, he observed, consistently stand in the way.

The first is a planning challenge rooted in the pace of change itself. Technology cycles have compressed to the point where a roadmap selected today may be superseded within six months. For institutions built around multi-year planning programs, this creates a disorienting vacuum: how do you commit resources to a direction when the landscape is shifting faster than your governance cycle?

The second is a fundamental paradigm shift in how AI applications are built. Unlike traditional software development, building effective AI systems requires validating what the model can actually do before designing the product around its capabilities — not the reverse. Teams that first design workflows and then assume AI will fill the gaps tend to discover those assumptions are wrong at precisely the wrong moment.

The third barrier, and perhaps the most underestimated, is organizational. Zhang Ning was direct on this point: AI projects led solely by technology teams tend to fail. The ones that deliver are invariably initiated from the business side, with genuine collaboration between business owners, product managers, and engineers from the outset. "This is fundamentally a cultural transformation," he noted, "not a technology project."

Data and Talent: The Hidden Foundations

On the data layer, Zhang Ning drew a distinction that deserves wider attention. Financial institutions, he argued, need to move beyond the traditional data warehouse toward what he describes as a "Knowledge Lake" — a structured repository designed specifically for AI agents. The goal is to convert internal documents, business rules, regulatory frameworks, and domain expertise into knowledge that AI systems can directly access and apply. This, not raw data volume, is what separates institutions capable of deploying effective AI agents from those that are not. The knowledge architecture has to be built for the agent, not retrofitted to it.

On talent, his view cuts against the conventional approach: AI capability is not primarily cultivated through training programs. It is identified through practice. Institutions that create cultures of broad experimentation — tolerating failure, rewarding learning — tend to discover their AI-capable talent organically through the process. The goal is to recognize people with genuine AI systems thinking and place them at the center of transformation efforts.

Yusys Technologies has translated these principles into deployed products. In one credit due diligence application, a system built on four specialized AI agents — covering information extraction, document verification, analytical computation, and report generation — has compressed the loan appraisal cycle from two weeks to half a day, delivering a 168-fold improvement in processing efficiency.

Rethinking the Legacy Problem

Andy Wu, General Manager of Yusys Technologies Hong Kong, offered a complementary diagnosis from the business transformation side. His central argument was a direct challenge to how the industry has been framing the legacy infrastructure problem.

Andy Wu, General Manager of Yusys Technologies Hong Kong, at the "Banking on Digital Transformation 101" panel

"The barrier isn't the age of the code," he said. "It's scenario rigidity." Core banking systems were architected for a world of predictable, human-initiated transactions. They were never designed for high-frequency, machine-driven financial activity. The implication is significant: replacing old code with new code does not resolve the underlying architectural mismatch. What is needed is a parallel modular approach — layering new AI capabilities alongside stable core systems rather than attempting wholesale replacement that carries operational risk and rarely delivers on its timeline.

On customer experience, Andy illustrated what genuine AI-driven personalization looks like in practice. Through Agentic AI that integrates cross-channel behavioral data, relationship managers can receive a three-second AI-generated briefing before a client call — covering not just account balances, but an inference about the client's likely intent based on their recent ecosystem behavior. The shift this enables is profound: from "here is a loan product that may interest you" to "here is a solution for the home we understand you are considering buying." Transaction-level personalization gives way to life-stage prediction.

On regulation — typically framed as a constraint on innovation — Andy offered an inversion of the standard narrative. The institutions genuinely leading the field across Asia, he observed, are frequently not those with the most sophisticated technology stack. They are the ones that have built what he termed "sovereign-ready RegTech" capability: the infrastructure to rapidly adapt to the compliance requirements of different regulatory jurisdictions without friction. Compliance designed into the architecture from the outset becomes a competitive accelerant, not a brake.

Looking toward 2030, Andy outlined a structural shift already underway. Banks, he argued, are evolving toward a model of "APIs and licenses" — becoming the invisible foundational infrastructure of a broader economic ecosystem. As tokenization extends beyond financial assets to encompass digital identity, tokenized real estate, and environmental credits, banks that position themselves as ecosystem orchestrators will define the next decade of financial services.

Yusys Technologies at Money20/20 Asia

Beyond the panel sessions, the Company hosted a booth at the exhibition that drew sustained interest from financial institutions across Southeast Asia, Europe, and the Middle East. Featured solutions included the Company's Due Diligence AI Agent — supporting multilingual document processing and seamless integration with existing loan management systems — and its Full-Stack AI Architecture, an open ecosystem connecting compute infrastructure, model layers, and financial use cases in a unified platform. Yusys Technologies also highlighted its ongoing collaboration with Huawei's RongHai initiative, which supports the localized delivery of AI-enabled financial solutions across international markets.

The Deeper Point

Across both panel sessions, a consistent argument emerged. The distance between AI as a concept and AI as a genuine business capability is not primarily technical. It is organizational, cultural, and architectural. The financial institutions that close that distance fastest will be those that approach AI not as a technology initiative to be managed within existing structures, but as a fundamental reorientation of how decisions are made, how customers are served, and how value is created.

That reorientation — from infrastructure to impact — is precisely what Yusys Technologies has built its international strategy around enabling.

This article was originally published by Yusys Technologies Co., Ltd. Any reproduction, republication, or quotation of this content — in whole or in part — must credit the source as "Yusys Technologies" and link to this page. Unauthorized use is prohibited.

🔒 Privacy Policy

Last updated: May 1, 2026

Yusys Technologies Co., Ltd. (hereinafter referred to as "Yusys") is committed to respecting and protecting your privacy. This commitment forms the foundation of our business activities and is regarded as our social responsibility and obligation. Consequently, when you visit our website, we will make every reasonable effort to help you maintain control over your personal data on the internet.

1. Collection of Your Information

To provide you with better services, we may request your name (or designation) and contact details in circumstances such as when you make an inquiry or submit a suggestion, thereby enabling us to provide you with rapid, accurate, and high-quality service.

2. Use of Your Information

The information you provide will be kept strictly confidential. Yusys will use your information solely to better understand your needs and to provide you with improved services. In certain instances, we may also use your information to contact you for market research or to provide you with market information which we believe may be of interest to you.

3. Control Over Your Information

When you provide your information to us through registration or other means, Yusys will not sell or rent your information to others. Your permission is always of paramount priority. We will not disclose your information to any third party that does not represent us, unless required by laws, regulations, or regulatory authorities in accordance with the law. We will not arbitrarily use or share the information you have provided to us in a manner unrelated to the purposes described above without your prior knowledge and consent.

4. Access to or Modification of Your Information

To protect your privacy and security, Yusys will take reasonable measures to verify your identity before granting you access to or the ability to modify your information. We strive to maintain the accuracy of your information and have provided you with the capability to access your information and registration data online, allowing you to update or modify your information. Please note that links provided on our website to third-party websites are offered solely as a convenience to you. If you use these links, you will leave the website of Yusys Technologies Co., Ltd., and such third-party websites may collect personally identifiable information about you. Therefore, our website does not endorse, nor makes any representations or warranties regarding, any third-party websites, their content, software, other products, materials, or any results obtained from using them.

5. Security of Your Information

We are committed to diligently protecting your data against loss, misuse, and unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction. Your data will be stored on servers controlled by passwords, thus any access to your data will be restricted and strictly protected.

6. Disclosure of Your Information

When disclosure of your information is required by regulatory authorities in accordance with legal procedures, Yusys will provide such information as mandated or for public security purposes. Under such circumstances, Yusys shall be exempt from liability, and you shall not hold Yusys accountable in any way.

7. Use of Cookies

With the application of new internet technologies, we offer a fully personalized experience to its visitors. We may use Cookie technology to analyze webpage usage patterns. Cookies are small files which, when placed on your device, enable us to provide certain features and functionality. The use of this technology does not mean that we automatically acquire any information about you. While Yusys may determine the type of computer you use, the primary purpose of using Cookies is to enhance your experience when visiting the our website. You retain complete autonomy to set your web browser (e.g., Microsoft Internet Explorer or Netscape Navigator) to notify you of Cookie placement requests, or to refuse Cookies entirely. You may also delete files containing Cookies; these files are saved as part of your internet browser.

8. Revisions to This Statement

The right to interpret this Statement is vested in Yusys Technologies Co., Ltd. Yusys reserves the right to update and amend this Privacy Statement at any time. In the event of material updates or changes to this Statement, Yusys will notify users by posting an announcement in a prominent position on its website.

9. Governing Law

Any disputes arising from this Statement or the use of this website shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the People's Republic of China.

The articles reproduced by our company are current-event articles on political and economic issues that have already been published on publicly accessible networks. They are reproduced solely for market research, discussion, and exchange purposes (with sources duly indicated). Should any objections arise, please promptly contact this website. We will, in accordance with relevant laws, remove or otherwise handle the articles as required.

Our company has consistently upheld a strong commitment to intellectual property protection and adheres to the provisions of all relevant Chinese intellectual property laws, regulations, and binding normative instruments. If any party believes that content published on this website may potentially infringe upon their right of communication through information networks, they should promptly notify our company via email, telephone, or written notice, providing corresponding proof of identity, proof of ownership, and detailed evidence of infringement. Upon receipt of the aforementioned legal documents, we will expeditiously remove the relevant content in accordance with the law and take appropriate actions.
